text:
Once upon an autumn evening, in a little village nestled between whispering forests and babbling brooks, there lived a wise old owl named Orion. Orion loved nothing more than to sit by his cozy fire at the highest perch in his oak tree, listening to the tales of far-off lands and strange creatures.

One sunny morning, Orion heard a soft tapping on his window branch. Peering outside, he saw a small, wise-looking bat with big round eyes. It chirped, "Oh dear, dear Orion. I've journeyed through darkness and lightning, heard secrets of the night, but nothing beats a good talk."

Orion chuckled. "Well then, tell me, little adventurer. What secrets have you heard?"

The bat fluttered closer. "About a mysterious garden hidden deep within the forest. Flowers never fade, berries never sour, and stars in the sky seem brighter. You could spend a lifetime there, hearing stories from the stars themselves."

Orion nodded thoughtfully. "
